Operation 'trackEvent' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description mentions the 1,000 event name limit but lacks critical details about what inputs are expected (EventRequest structure), what the operation returns, and the side effect of real-time event persistence. Added specifics about input schema, return value, and real-time availability to improve AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Records a custom event with optional context data. Maximum 1,000 unique custom event names per project.
data.description_suggestion: Records a custom event with optional context data to track user interactions and behaviors. Accepts an EventRequest object containing event name, properties, and optional screen, page, or campaign context. Maximum 1,000 unique custom event names per project. Returns a success confirmation. Events are persisted in real-time and available for analysis in GoSquared dashboards.
data.reasoning: The current description mentions the 1,000 event name limit but lacks critical details about what inputs are expected (EventRequest structure), what the operation returns, and the side effect of real-time event persistence. Added specifics about input schema, return value, and real-time availability to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'trackPageview' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. The current description is vague about inputs and outputs. It doesn't specify what PageviewRequest contains, what the operation returns, or the side effects of recording pageviews. Enhanced description includes expected input structure, return value, and clarifies that pageviews enable journey analytics and session tracking, making it more actionable for AI agents.

data.current_description: Records a pageview event to track what pages a visitor is browsing across your site.
data.description_suggestion: Records a pageview event to track visitor browsing behavior across your site. Accepts a PageviewRequest object containing page URL, title, and optional context such as referrer, screen dimensions, and campaign information. Returns a success confirmation. Pageview data is persisted in real-time and used to build visitor journey analytics and session tracking.
data.reasoning: The current description is vague about inputs and outputs. It doesn't specify what PageviewRequest contains, what the operation returns, or the side effects of recording pageviews. Enhanced description includes expected input structure, return value, and clarifies that pageviews enable journey analytics and session tracking, making it more actionable for AI agents.
